$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports verbatim.

TypeScript port. Drops the block-library-internal `useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps` hook (ToolsPanel falls back to its default dropdown). The icon button's aria-label falls back buttonText â†’ label â†’ 'Search' so the canvas matches the renderers' #338 a11y output. Native branches dropped (web-only).
core/search ships no save (rendered outside save); the fork returns null so the block serializes to its delimiter and the front-end renderers build the form from attributes.
Upstream has no deprecated.js; empty array exported for symmetry.
Upstream has no transforms.js; the fork adds bidirectional core/search â†” artisanpack/search block transforms for the rollout window.

Body is a verbatim port of the single default variation seeding buttonText/label with 'Search'; the fork adds a JSDoc file-header pointing at the upstream source so the lineage is visible without consulting this state file. No semantic drift from upstream.

Body is a verbatim port of the width constants + isPercentageUnit helper; the fork adds a JSDoc file-header pointing at the upstream source so the lineage is visible without consulting this state file. No semantic drift from upstream.
Minimal editor-canvas affordances; the bulk of styling comes from the shared block-library stylesheet because the fork reuses the wp-block-search* class names.
Inline SVG mirroring @wordpress/icons' `search` icon (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).

Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract. Upstream's privateApis content-only inspector fields (fieldsKey/formKey behind window.__experimentalContentOnlyInspectorFields) and PHP-side init wiring are not carried.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx drops the upstream `useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps` hook (a block-library internal not exposed via exports). The ToolsPanel uses its default dropdown behaviour.
edit.tsx labels the icon button via buttonText â†’ label â†’ 'Search', matching the renderers' #338 fix, where upstream falls back only buttonText â†’ 'Search'.
save returns null (dynamic); the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers, which reuse the core/search renderers carrying the #338 fix.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream â€” required to coexist with core/search during the V1 rollout.
index.ts drops upstream's privateApis content-only inspector fields and PHP-side init wiring.
